Options lack distinctness - 'fly ball approach' and 'look for a pitch to drive' are too similar as both involve swinging
Scenario unrealistic - closers rarely enter with runner on 3rd and 1 out in save situations
Best explanation focuses too much on why alternatives fail rather than teaching the fly ball technique itself
Age-inappropriate complexity - discussing '96-mph closer' and 'margin for error' for minimum age 12
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Rewrite explanations to flow naturally without formulaic transitions. Focus the best explanation on HOW to execute a fly ball approach (hands back, slight uppercut, aim for outfield). Make options more distinct by replacing 'work the count' with a different strategic choice like 'choke up for contact'.

MEDIUM.title: Fast Runner — Can You Turn Two?
issues: Best answer rate (85) doesn't match best option index (option 1 has rate 30, but option 2 has rate 85)
Explanations use formulaic transitions ('The reason:', 'This approach doesn't match the situation')
Option 3 explanation is vague and doesn't teach a specific principle
Several explanations focus on why NOT to do something rather than teaching positive principles
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Fix rate array so option 2 (index 1) has highest rate. Rewrite explanations to flow naturally without formulaic phrases, teaching positive principles for each choice.

MEDIUM.title: Cutoff Man Position on a Relay
issues: Explanations contain formulaic 'The reason:' structure which breaks natural flow
Runner on 1st with a double would likely score easily - situation lacks realism for a relay home
Best explanation focuses too much on mechanics rather than strategic decision-making
Option 2 (third base) might actually be better since runner from 1st can't realistically be thrown out at home
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Change situation to runner on 2nd (not 1st) so relay home makes strategic sense. Rewrite explanations to remove formulaic transitions and focus on WHY home vs third is the right strategic choice given the game situation.

MEDIUM.title: Intentional Walk
issues: Explanations use formulaic 'since this approach doesn't match the situation' repeatedly, violating specific teaching requirement
Score perspective error: Bot 9 means HOME team bats, but score [5,4] suggests away winning by 1
Explanation 0 uses awkward 'The reason:' structure instead of natural flow
Best answer explanation focuses on why (strikeouts) but doesn't teach the broader DP setup principle
Option 3 'Walk both' is unclear - walk current batter AND next?
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Fix score to [4,5] for home trailing. Rewrite explanations: 'With runners on 2nd and 3rd, you create force plays at every base while facing a weaker hitter who strikes out 25% of the time.' Clarify option 3 as 'Walk both batters intentionally'.

MEDIUM.title: Tag Up on a Fly Ball
issues: Explanations use formulaic 'since this approach doesn't match the situation' repeatedly, failing to teach specific principles
Wrong explanations (0,2,3) don't teach WHY those choices are wrong with baseball reasoning
Explanation 1 uses awkward 'This is because' structure mid-sentence
Count shows 1-0 but isn't relevant to a tag-up rule scenario
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Replace generic 'doesn't match the situation' phrases with specific teaching: explain option 0 violates the tag-up rule, option 2 wastes precious time, and option 3 misunderstands that fly balls allow advancement after catches

MEDIUM.title: Hit and Run
issues: Diff:1 scenario for ages 11+ is inconsistent - diff:1 should be ages 6-8
Explanation 2 uses vague phrase 'doesn't match the situation' without teaching specific principle
Explanation 4 contains awkward mid-sentence transition 'because the downside outweighs'
Hit-and-run execution might be too advanced for the youngest players in age 11+ range
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Change to diff:2 with ageMin:9, rewrite explanation 2 to teach 'Being selective leaves the runner exposed to an easy throw from catcher to second', and smooth explanation 4 to 'Pulling for power risks strikeouts when you need contact to protect the runner.'

MEDIUM.title: Pitcher Hit Your Teammate — Stay Focused
issues: Explanations contain incomplete sentences and fragments ('which means get a hit', 'because stay in your normal position')
Option 2 explanation uses 'Don't let the pitcher live rent-free in your head' which may be too idiomatic for 9-year-olds
Explanations for options 2 and 3 don't follow complete sentence structure
The scenario teaches emotional control more than situational hitting as tagged
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Rewrite explanations as complete sentences: Option 2: 'Taking every pitch lets the pitcher off the hook. Getting a hit hurts him more than drawing a walk.' Option 3: 'Crowding the plate after an HBP is asking to get hit yourself. Stay in your normal batting position and focus on hitting.'

MEDIUM.title: Bases Loaded, Cleanup Spot — Smart Approach
issues: Explanations contain incomplete sentences and fragments ('since you're trying to do too much', 'which means if he grooves one, crush it', 'because drive the ball')
Multiple explanations use informal language inappropriate for teaching ('is insane', 'crush it')
Third explanation contradicts itself - says being patient wastes pressure but then suggests crushing a grooved pitch
Fourth explanation is poorly written with grammar errors ('because drive the ball' is not a complete thought)
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Rewrite all explanations as complete, grammatically correct sentences. For example: 'Bunting with bases loaded as the cleanup hitter wastes your power and the scoring opportunity, since even a routine fly ball or ground ball can score runs with the bases full.'

MEDIUM.title: Double Play Depth
issues: Explanations 2-4 use formulaic 'since this approach doesn't match the situation' endings that violate natural language flow requirements
Wrong answer explanations don't teach specific principles - just state generically that they 'don't match'
Concept description is overly technical for diff:1/ages 6-8 ('DP' abbreviation, technical positioning details)
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Rewrite explanations 2-4 to teach specific lessons: #2 could explain why range matters less with runner on 1st, #3 could teach when deep positioning IS useful, #4 could explain what info you'd need before shifting. Use simpler language throughout for age 6+.

MEDIUM.title: Left-Handed Pitcher on the Mound
issues: Explanations contain grammatical errors and incomplete sentences ('because learn to read the move')
Option 3 explanation contradicts itself - says huge lead is bad but doesn't explain why it's an option
Explanations don't teach the breakeven concept mentioned in conceptTag

MEDIUM.title: One Run Game, 8th Inning — Bunt to Third?
issues: Explanation 3 contains an incomplete sentence fragment ('since think about what gives your team') that breaks grammatical flow
Explanation 4 uses formulaic structure 'The reason:' which violates natural language flow requirements
Option 4 'Take a pitch first, then decide' is too passive/generic for a manager scenario - should be a concrete strategic decision
Explanations don't fully explore the statistical/analytical angle expected at difficulty 3 (ages 12+)
MEDIUM.suggestedFix: Rewrite explanation 3 to complete the thought: 'A hit and run with a runner on second is risky because the runner has a long way to go and your best hitter might have to swing at a bad pitch.' Rewrite explanation 4 more naturally: 'Taking a pitch doesn't change the fundamental decision - your #3 hitter should be swinging in this high-leverage situation.' Replace option 4 with a more concrete managerial decision like 'Pinch hit for more power'.

MEDIUM.title: 2-2 — Protect or Attack?
issues: Explanation 3 uses 'If it's off the plate, fight it off' which contradicts looking for fastballs - should clarify protection vs. attack zones

MEDIUM.title: Bases Loaded — Throw Home
issues: Explanation 3 uses generic 'doesn't match the situation' phrase instead of teaching specific principle
No explanation teaches WHY home-to-first double play is easier/more likely than second-to-first
Runner positions should be [1,2,3] not [1,2,3] - needs clearer notation
